_comment: v1.4.1 — scripted LLM responses for the cockpit e2e specs. The fixture is bind-mounted into portal-csr via docker-compose.e2e.yml and selected by BSS_LLM_FIXTURE_PATH. orchestrator/bss_orchestrator/llm_mock.py owns the read + match logic. Match strings are case-insensitive substring searches against the latest user message.

responses.name: tool-roundtrip-customer-list
responses._comment: Spec test_cockpit_tool_roundtrip — exercises the LLM → tool → render loop. customer.list against a name_contains filter is the cheapest read tool that returns a stable rendering, and it doesn't require a known customer_id (the e2e spec just needs SOMETHING to come back; the seeded demo customers cover this when make demo-restore has run).

responses.name: propose-then-confirm-destructive
responses._comment: Spec test_cockpit_propose_then_confirm — verifies the destructive-tool gate proposes (doesn't execute) until /confirm fires. provisioning.set_fault_injection is in DESTRUCTIVE_TOOLS but has the cheapest blast radius (single config row in a sim service). The args target a non-existent service so /confirm executes against the real provisioning-sim and returns an error — but the spec only asserts the proposal UI appeared and was consumed by /confirm, not the tool's success.

responses.name: knowledge-uncited-fallback
responses._comment: Spec test_cockpit_knowledge_citation_or_fallback — the cockpit's hallucination guard (_RE_KNOWLEDGE_CLAIM) replaces uncited handbook claims with the canonical fallback string. Mock returns a single content message with the trigger phrase 'per the handbook' and NO knowledge.* tool call. The guard fires; the spec asserts the fallback text is present.

steps.content: Per the handbook, refunds are issued at the operator's discretion within 30 days of purchase.
